And essentially this code is what identifies your ad account. You'll see here this number here that's blued out a little bit, 834683. This is our pixel ID and our pixel ID identifies the specific ad account tied to that pixel or whatever ad account we've assigned to it. ## [03:02 - 06:03]

that you're doing. So this base pixel here allows you to track essentially the audiences or the people that are hitting your website. However, the base pixel itself does not allow you to track specific actions other than page view as you'll see here such as purchases or add to cart or opt ins or quote requests. That's where we're going to get into event codes. Step number two, so step number one is just a base pixel and that really identifies the pixel and the ad account and it ties it, sorry, it ties it back to the ad account and it also allows you to create an audience of everyone that triggered page view so you can actually retarget just with the base pixel. However, it won't let you track specific things like purchases or opt ins. And the way that you would install this is you'd simply copy this and you'd install it between the head tags of your website. So you'll see here between the header tags of your website. If you're on a ClickFunnels landing page for example, what you can do is go under tracking code and you'll see here you can just paste in that code directly in here. If you're on WordPress, I would recommend that you download the plugin called Pixel Your Site and Pixel Your Site, let me show you. Now with this advanced matching, don't worry too much about this, but what this essentially allows advertisers to do is if you click it, you'll see it just creates another line here where it passes more data back to Facebook.
